Vice (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $47,836,282Details
International Box Office $23,057,500
Worldwide Box Office $70,893,782
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,046,114
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,360,957
Total $2,407,071
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The epic story of how a bureaucratic Washington insider quietly became the most powerful man in the world as Vice-President to George W. Bush, reshaping the country and the globe in ways that we still feel today.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,768,371 (16.2% of total gross)
Legs:6.16 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:67.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$60,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,442 opening theaters/2,534 max. theaters, 5.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $62,600,446

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2018 (Wide) by Annapurna Pictures
International Releases: December 21st, 2018 (Wide) (Australia)
January 3rd, 2019 (Wide), released as Vice - L'uomo nell'ombra (Italy)
January 11th, 2019 (Wide), released as El Vicio del poder (Spain)
January 25th, 2019 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
January 25th, 2019 (Wide) (Argentina)
Video Release: March 12th, 2019 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language and some violent images.
(Rating bulletin 2548 (Cert #51824), 10/10/2018)
Running Time: 132 minutes
Award nomination: 2019 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Plot point: Biography, Politicians
Subgenre: Black Comedy
Time period setting: 2000s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Black Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Plan B Entertainment, Gary Sanchez Productions, Annapurna Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

The epic story of how a bureaucratic Washington insider quietly became the most powerful man in the world as Vice-President to George W. Bush, reshaping the country and the globe in ways that we still feel today.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,768,371 (16.2% of total gross)
Legs:6.16 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:67.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$60,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,442 opening theaters/2,534 max. theaters, 5.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $62,600,446

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2018 (Wide) by Annapurna Pictures

January 25th, 2019 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
